MINI MINUTES FOR APR-02-2015 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES IS: GRANTED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION #3 (DRIVER'S LICENSES, VOTER REGISTRATION CARDS, PASSPORTS) IS OVERBROAD AS TO SCOPE AND TIME AND IS DENIED. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION #4 (DOCUMENTS EVIDENCING PLACES OF DOMICILE AND RESIDENCE FOR PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S WIFE FOR 2004 TO PRESENT) IS RELEVANT BUT OVERBROAD. THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED ARE LIMITED TO THOSE WHICH IDENTIFY ANY PLACE OF RESIDENCE FOR PLAINTIFF DURING THE FOUR YEARS PRIOR TO OCTOBER 9, 2013. THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED ARE FURTHER LIMITED TO THOSE WHICH SET FORTH OR DOCUMENT PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO OCCUPY THE PLACE OF RESIDENCE SUCH AS A LETTER OF PERMISSION, GRANT DEED, LEASE OR RENTAL AGREEMENT. REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION #5 (DOCUMENTS EVIDENCING REAL ESTATE OWNED BY PLAINTIFF AND OR PLAINTIFF'S WIFE) IS OVERBROAD SINCE IT HAS NO REQUIREMENT OF RESIDENCE BY PLAINTIFF AND IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: SANFORD KINGSLEY. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE CALENDAR FOR ATTORNEY JOHN F. CAMBRIA
Off Calendar Feb-26-2016 Renewal Fees Paid

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Plaintiffs Designated Expert Witness Phillip Gay
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS J. LOSAVIO, ESQ. of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. 415-981-6630 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. RICHARD E. LEVINE, ESQ. of Levine & Baker LLP, Tel No. 415-391-8177 appearing for the Defendant Tavistock Restaurants, LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S Motion To Disqualify Plaintiffs Designated Expert Witness Phillip Gay is denied. Defendant does not assert that there is any evidentiary privilege that applies to Mr. Gay and thus Mr. Gay could have been called as a percipient witness as to all matters which defendant claims are confidential. Moreover, although the parties' papers are not precise on this point, it appears that defendant has produced documents in this case covering much or all of the claimed confidential information that Mr. Gay may testify about and thus any expert retained by plaintiff would have access to the same information. In all events, to the extent that Mr. Gay seeks to testify about matters that he learned while he was a consultant for defendant that are not relevant to the issues in the case or the disqualifying factors of Evidence Code 352 substantially outweigh the probative value of such evidence, defendant may seek to exclude such evidence by motion in limine and/or objections at the trial. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JOHN F. CAMBRIA
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Continued To Feb-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas LoSavio of Low, Ball & Lynch telephone number 415-981-6630 for Plaintiff.
Richard Levine of Levine & Baker LLP telephone number 415-391-8177 for Defendant Tavistock Restaurants.
John Cambria of Alston & Bird telephone number 212-210-9583 appeared by telephone for Defendant Tavistock Restaurants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court maintained its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - Defendant Tavistock's Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to Plaintiff's claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress. The California Supreme Court sets a "high bar" for what can constitute severe distress. (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1051.) "Severe emotional distress means ' "emotional distress of such substantial quality or enduring quality that no reasonable [person] in civilized society should be expected to endure it." ' [Citations.]" (Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 1004.) Here, Plaintiff has not met his burden for his claims for emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress. Defendant requested changing the locks on their corporate apartment after terminating Plaintiff. Defendant granted access to Plaintiff's wife on the only occasion that she or Plaintiff requested access to the apartment. Even if Plaintiff's wife was "uncomfortable and upset" because two men accompanied her when she visited the apartment, that is not beyond the bounds of decency (Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts, 48.)
Defendant Tavistock's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to Plaintiff's claims for breach of contract and specific performance. Whether there was an oral agreement at the airport is an issue of fact. Evidence in support of an agreement includes Mr. Thakkar's letter that rescinds an offer for stock options. Evidence in support of no agreement includes Plaintiff's letter that Mr. Thakkar's offer for stock options was forthcoming. That Mr. Thakkar handed Plaintiff a Draft Severance Agreement that Defendant did not sign does not disprove an oral agreement. If there was an oral contract, whether that contract contravenes the Operating Agreement is not a question of law requiring summary adjudication. A proposed order was provided for the Court's review. The Court indicated that it may add language to the order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant To Appear For Deposition And To Produce Documents
Off Calendar Apr-02-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Futher Responses
Off Calendar Apr-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Levine of Levine & Baker LLP telephone number 415-391-8177 for Defendants.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Counsel for Defendants stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses is: GRANTED in part as follows: Request for Production #3 (driver's licenses, voter registration cards, passports) is overbroad as to scope and time and is denied. Request for Production #4 (documents evidencing places of domicile and residence for Plaintiff and Plaintiff's wife for 2004 to present) is relevant but overbroad. The documents to be produced are limited to those which identify any place of residence for Plaintiff during the four years prior to October 9, 2013. The documents to be produced are further limited to those which set forth or document Plaintiff's right to occupy the place of residence such as a letter of permission, grant deed, lease or rental agreement. Request for Production #5 (documents evidencing real estate owned by Plaintiff and or Plaintiff's wife) is overbroad since it has no requirement of residence by Plaintiff and is denied. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kingsley. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS J. LOSAVIO of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. (415) 981-6630 appearing for Plaintiff Bryan Lockwood;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. APPLICATION OF JOHN F. CAMBRIA, ESQ. FOR ADMISSION PRO HAC VICE AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S is granted. No opposition filed. The motion complies with CRC 9.40. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. THOMAS J. LOSAVIO of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. (415) 981-6630 appearing for Plaintiff Bryan Lockwood;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads the elements of breach of an oral contract. (FAC, pars. 6, 10, 12-13.) This cause of action is not uncertain. Rare One LLC is not an indispensable party. The FAC pleads that Plaintiff and his wife control Rare One LLC. (FAC, par. 2.) As such, Plaintiff can protect Rare One LLC's interests. (See CCP sec. 389; Citizens Assn. for Sensible Development of Bishop Area v. County of Inyo (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 151, 161.) The FAC also adequately pleads the elements of specific performance. (FAC, pars. 14-21; see Coodwest Rubber Corp. v. Munoz (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 919, 921.) Defendant's request for judicial notice of the four LLC Operating Agreements and one unexecuted Agreement and Release is denied. The terms and legal effect of a contract are not facts that are not reasonably subject to dispute. (See Evid. Code sec. 452(h).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
